answer:
Fix it. Kill only if fixes fail in 60–90 days.

Numbers: LTV:CAC = 2.2 — below the 3:1 threshold but not dead. Payback = $900 ÷ $150 = 6 months (better on gross margin basis: ~7–7.5 months at 80% margin). Acceptable for B2B SaaS if cash allows; too thin to scale.

At $6k/mo you're buying ~6–7 customers/mo — small sample, so CAC estimate is noisy. Don't overreact to one month.

Fix priorities:
1. Cut to exact-match/high-intent keywords; kill broad match and Display/Search Partners. Typical 20–40% waste there.
2. Segment CAC by campaign/keyword — usually 20% of spend drives most conversions. Kill the rest.
3. Switch bidding to tCPA/tROAS with offline conversion import (closed-won, not form fills) so Google optimizes for revenue, not leads.
4. Landing page CVR: every point of improvement drops CAC proportionally.
5. Raise LTV: annual prepay discount, expansion pricing — moving churn to push LTV to $2.5–3k fixes the ratio without touching ads.

Decision rule: if CAC ≤ ~$650 (3:1) or payback ≤ 5 months within 90 days, scale spend. If it stays >$900 after fixes, reallocate to channels with better B2B economics (outbound, partnerships, SEO).
